title: atriplex vesicaria subsp. calcicola
intro: Atriplex vesicaria subsp. calcicola is a subspecies of the Atriplex vesicaria plant, commonly known as saltbush, which is a type of succulent shrub native to the coastal regions of Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat skin conditions, wounds, and digestive issues, due to its high content of antioxidants and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Atriplex vesicaria subsp. calcicola include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe and protect the skin, as well as its potential to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and diabetes. Atriplex vesicaria subsp. calcicola can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional Australian Aboriginal medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
